title: Ex-Sotheby’s CEO Tad Smith Banks on NFTs, Agrees to Buy Collectibles Platform Candy Digital
description: The deal has not closed, but Smith announced on X that he would serve as CEO.

title: Manhattan D.A.’s Office Returns 17 Stolen Antiquities and Rare Books to Italy
description: The recovered items were formally returned in a ceremony held this week

title: Long-Lost Skeleton of D’Artagnan, the Fourth Musketeer, Discovered in Dutch Church
description: The remains, along with a musket ball and a 17th-century French coin, were found under the floor of a church in Maastricht.

title: Paul Klee Painting Once Owned by Walter Benjamin Thwarted from US Debut Because of Mideast War
description: The work is currently still at the Israel Museum, which had agreed to loan it to a show at the Jewish Museum in New York.

title: Hong Kong Signs Five-Year Agreement to Keep Hosting Art Basel Fair
description: The deal also calls for Art Basel to expand the fair in both scale and impact.

title: The Best Booths at Art Basel Hong Kong, From AI Magical Girls to Asia’s Unsung Masters
description: In a strong year for curation across the fair, pairing of historic and emerging artists from Asia shine.

title: UK Government Plans to Charge Admission to National Museums for International Visitors
description: The policy change is meant to help fund greater access to the arts in the country.

title: Ukrainian Official Calls For Russia to be Banned from Venice Biennale After Drone Strike Damages UNESCO-Listed Monastery
description: “Get real!” said minister of foreign affairs Andrii Sybiha in a post on X.

title: Marica Vilcek, Art Historian Whose Foundation Upheld the Work of Immigrants, Dies at 89
description: Countless artists and curators have received large sums from her foundation.

title: Following Shutdown of AI Video Platform Sora, Disney Pulls Out of $1 B. Deal with OpenAI
description: In the deal, which was signed last year, Disney had agreed to license some of its characters for use in Sora.
